input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're most likely missing the `BindingContext`. The page doesn't know which object to bind against. Set it in your code-behind:

```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or inherits from `ObservableObject` in the MVVM Community Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change.

Alternatively, you can set `BindingContext` directly in XAML:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. Their XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but the labels are blank despite the ViewModel having values.

The most likely issue is that they haven't set the `BindingContext` of the page to their ViewModel. Without a `BindingContext`, the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

Another possible issue is that the ViewModel properties don't raise `PropertyChanged` notifications (i.e., don't implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`), but the most common cause of *completely* blank labels is a missing `BindingContext`.


I should provide a straightforward solution that addresses the most likely culprit first, then mention the secondary possibility.
